channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Divide Among Supreme Court’s Conservatives Could Test Trump’s Agenda
item.description: In rejecting President Trump’s tariffs, the court’s six conservative justices displayed subtle differences in their views of executive power.

item.title: If the recent AI and crypto shocks upset you, you’re tracking the wrong cycle
item.description: Claude and DeepSeek, separated at birth?

item.title: The CEO of a $1 billion AI unicorn says his peers in Silicon Valley want you to fear for your job, but they’re actually first on the chopping block
item.description: Tanmai Gopal of PromptQL said tech people have a bad habit of thinking "this affects me. So it's going to affect everyone like that."

item.title: ‘I have a chip on my shoulder.’ Phoebe Gates wants her $185 million AI startup Phia to succeed with ‘no ties to my privilege or my last name’
item.description: The daughter of Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates acknowledges her privilege, but says she’s building her own thing without their help.

item.title: Blizzard Warnings Issued for Swath of East Coast, Including N.Y.C.
item.description: The blizzard warnings are the first since 2017 for New York City, which is expected to get up to 18 inches of snow, or more.

item.title: Peter Thiel and other tech billionaires are publicly shielding their children from the products that made them rich
item.description: As governments race to restrict minors’ social media use, the tech billionaires who built the platforms are imposing strict screen limits at home.

item.title: How fandom became culture’s power center — and a blueprint for Gen Z’s economic influence
item.description: Fandoms sit at the intersection of identity, influence, and spending. Three dynamics explain their accelerating economic power.

item.title: How Johannes Klaebo’s 6 Gold Medals Ranks Among the All-Time Olympic Performances
item.description: The Norwegian cross-country skier completed the biggest medal haul ever by an athlete at a Winter Games, and one of the biggest at any single Olympics...

item.title: Bridge Owner Donated $1 Million to MAGA Group Before Trump Blasted Competitor
item.description: The PAC and the White House say the donation had nothing to do with President Trump’s tirade against a new bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Onta...

item.title: Los Angeles Olympics Have a Cloud of Concerns as Winter Games Close
item.description: New details about the LA28 chairman’s ties to Jeffrey Epstein, as well as anxiety over key infrastructure projects, have thrown the planning into disa...

item.title: Los Angeles Olympics Have a Cloud of Concerns as Winter Games Close
item.description: New details about the LA28 chairman’s ties to Jeffrey Epstein, as well as anxiety over key infrastructure projects, have thrown the planning into disa...

item.title: How Johannes Klaebo’s 6 Gold Medals Ranks Among the All-Time Olympic Performances
item.description: The Norwegian cross-country skier completed the biggest medal haul ever by an athlete at a Winter Games, and one of the biggest at any single Olympics...

item.title: Ferreira completes his halfpipe set with gold.
item.description: The American Alex Ferreira nailed his final run to win gold in the ski halfpipe.

item.title: Searching for Rejuvenation Where Skiers Chase Olympic Gold
item.description: The thermal waters of the Italian Alps, long praised for their restorative properties, are well known to elite athletes. During the Winter Games, they...

item.title: Klaebo. Inevitable.
item.description: Johannes Klaebo won his sixth cross country gold medal at the 2026 Olympics.
